title: Kendrick Lamar and SZA Win Record of the Year at 2026 Grammys
text: Kendrick Lamar and SZA claimed the Record of the Year award at the 68th Grammy Awards for "Luther," their R&B ballad from Lamar's album GNX. The song defeated nominees including tracks by Bad Bunny, Sabrina Carpenter, Billie Eilish, and others. Cher presented the award to the artists and producers Sounwave and Jack Antonoff. During his acceptance speech, Lamar expressed deep admiration for the song's inspiration, stating: "Luther Vandross is one of my favorite artists of all time." He noted that Vandross's estate granted permission with a stipulation for "no cursing" on the track, which he described as a meaningful honor for the team. SZA used her moment to encourage listeners, saying: "We're not governed by the government. We're governed by God." The track samples a 1982 rendition by Vandross and Cheryl Lynn. Producer Sounwave revealed the song initially began as a simple interlude with just sampled elements and hummed melodies before evolving into a full production. "Luther" spent 13 consecutive weeks at number one on the Billboard Hot 100, making it the artists' longest-charting number-one collaboration and the second longest-running hip-hop song ever on that chart.
